Quorum wake occurs at six successes and a partial summary may be published with explicit caveats, while remaining unresolved identities stay unclosed.

At least one expected security_check_id reaches timed_out; reconciliation accounts for the terminal status, marks that identity unresolved for complete labeling, and then follows the still-open failure_policy.
A result or report contribution is observed for a security_check_id outside the launch manifest of 20 checks; reconciliation flags an unknown identity and then follows the still-open unknown_policy.
If partial_policy is reject, the control plane does not publish a partial report when some expected identities remain missing, failed, cancelled, or timed_out.
If partial_policy is allow_incomplete, the control plane may publish a partial report before full reconciliation, but it must not apply the complete label until all completion checks pass.

scenarios.expected: All 80 manifest identities are launched, each is represented exactly once with a terminal status, no unknown identities appear, and final reconciliation can mark the batch complete.
If one or more of the 80 expected identities lack terminal representation, await does not produce a complete report and reconciliation flags unresolved identities.
If any classification task identity is represented more than once, reconciliation detects duplicates and complete labeling is blocked pending the open duplicate policy.
If one or more classification tasks end with failed, reconciliation counts those terminal statuses and marks those identities unresolved pending the open failure policy.
If one or more classification tasks end with timed_out, reconciliation counts those terminal statuses and marks those identities unresolved pending the open failure policy.
If a terminal record arrives for an identity outside the 80-item manifest, reconciliation flags an unknown identity and complete labeling is blocked pending the open unknown policy.
If some identities are unresolved and policy is set to reject incomplete publication, the control plane withholds a complete result.
If some identities are unresolved and policy is set to allow_incomplete, the control plane may publish a partial result while still recording unresolved identities.

The design records the external API limits of 10 concurrent requests and 100 requests per minute as target data-plane constraints while keeping all 80 classification tasks logically independent in the control plane.

At least one of the 80 expected classification task identities has no terminal report; reconciliation marks unresolved identities and withholds the complete label.
A classification task identity is represented more than once in terminal reports; reconciliation detects duplicates and outcome remains policy-open pending duplicate_policy.

One or more classification task identities reach failed status; reconciliation counts terminal statuses, marks those identities unresolved for completeness, and final outcome remains policy-open pending failure_policy.

If business policy refuses partial publication, any unresolved identity prevents publication and the batch remains not complete.
If business policy allows incomplete publication, the control plane may publish a partial batch result while still marking reconciliation as not complete.

The design records external API limits of 10 concurrent requests and 100 requests per minute, with enforcement delegated to the target data plane while control-plane accounting still expects 80 identities.

scenarios.expected: With exactly one terminal record each for route-001 through route-060 and no extras, reconciliation passes all checks and the control plane may mark the batch complete.

Given 60 collector records where route-007 is absent and route-008 appears twice, reconciliation detects expected_vs_represented mismatch and unresolved identity route-007, so the batch cannot receive the complete label.

Given 60 collector records where route-008 appears twice, reconciliation detects a duplicate represented identity and the batch cannot receive the complete label regardless of unresolved duplicate policy.

If one expected route item reaches terminal status failed, terminal status accounting must include it and unresolved identities must include that route, so complete labeling is blocked unless policy later permits some incomplete publication path.

If one expected route item reaches terminal status timed_out, terminal status accounting must include it and unresolved identities must include that route, so complete labeling is blocked unless policy later permits some incomplete publication path.
If the collector includes a record for an identity outside route-001 through route-060, reconciliation detects an unknown identity and the batch cannot receive the complete label.

For the described batch with route-007 missing and route-008 duplicated, if partial publication is refused then the control plane records the reconciliation defects and withholds any publishable result.

For the described batch with route-007 missing and route-008 duplicated, if partial publication is allowed then the control plane may emit an incomplete or diagnostic result, but it still must withhold the complete label because missing and duplicate checks failed.

open_points.question: Should duplicate records for one expected route identity be rejected, tolerated, or otherwise handled during reconciliation?
Should records for identities outside the launch manifest be rejected or tolerated during reconciliation?
May the workflow publish a final result when one or more expected route identities end in failed, cancelled, or timed_out status?
May an incomplete result be published when expected route identities are missing or otherwise unresolved?

scenarios.expected: When route-001 through route-060 are each represented exactly once with terminal statuses accounted, reconciliation marks expected_vs_represented satisfied, finds no duplicates or unknown identities, finds no unresolved identities, and the batch is eligible for a complete label.

Given expected identities route-001 through route-060, if the collector receives 60 records but route-007 is absent, reconciliation flags route-007 as unresolved and complete-label requirements fail even if total record count equals 60.
Given the same collector snapshot, if route-008 appears twice, reconciliation flags a duplicate identity for route-008 and complete-label requirements fail regardless of the raw record count.

If any expected route identity has terminal status failed, reconciliation accounts for that terminal status and lists that identity as unresolved until failure_policy is decided; a complete label is not satisfied.

If any expected route identity has terminal status timed_out, reconciliation accounts for that terminal status and lists that identity as unresolved until failure_policy is decided; a complete label is not satisfied.
If the collector includes a record for an identity outside route-001 through route-060, reconciliation flags an unknown identity and complete-label requirements fail.

For the snapshot where route-007 is missing and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later set to reject, the control plane must refuse publication of a result because unresolved and duplicate conditions remain.

For the snapshot where route-007 is missing and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later set to allow_incomplete, the control plane may publish only an explicitly incomplete result while still surfacing unresolved route-007 and duplicate route-008 findings.

open_points.question: Should duplicate child results for the same expected identity be rejected or tolerated during reporting?
Should results carrying identities not present in the launch manifest be rejected from reporting or retained as exceptions?
Do failed or timed-out expected children prevent publishing a report, or may reporting proceed with incomplete/error-marked outcomes?
May a report be published when quorum wake occurs or reconciliation shows unresolved expected identities?

scenarios.expected: All 10 launched child identities eventually report terminal statuses with no duplicates and no unknown identities; quorum wake may occur earlier at 6 terminals, but final reconciliation marks the batch complete only after all 10 expected identities are represented and terminal-status-accounted.

In the described run, one result carries an unknown identity, so one expected launch-manifest identity remains unrepresented; final reconciliation fails expected_vs_represented and unresolved checks, and the batch cannot receive a complete label.

If any expected child identity is reported more than once, quorum wake may already have occurred, but final reconciliation flags duplicate identity presence; publication behavior remains open under duplicate_policy.

In the described run, 2 expected child identities reach failed terminal status; they count toward quorum readiness and terminal-status accounting, but they remain unresolved identities for complete-label purposes; publication impact remains open under failure_policy.

In the described run, 1 expected child identity reaches timed_out terminal status; it counts toward quorum readiness and terminal-status accounting, but it remains unresolved for complete-label purposes; publication impact remains open under failure_policy.

In the described run, 1 reported result has an identity absent from the launch manifest; quorum wake is based only on expected identities, and final reconciliation flags an unknown identity plus one missing expected identity; handling remains open under unknown_policy.

If partial reporting is not allowed, the described run wakes the control plane once 6 expected successes are terminal, but reporting is withheld after reconciliation because failed, timed_out, unknown, and missing/unresolved conditions prevent a complete label.

If partial reporting is allowed, the described run wakes the control plane at quorum 6 and may publish a partial report after reconciliation that explicitly indicates 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 unknown identity reported, and at least 1 expected identity unresolved/missing from represented identities.

The 1 timed-out expected child identity is counted as terminal_status_accounted but remains unresolved for complete labeling; whether timeout blocks reporting or allows partial publication is open under failure_policy.
The terminal report with an identity absent from the launch snapshot is flagged as unknown; complete labeling fails and publication outcome remains open under unknown_policy.

If partial_policy is later decided as reject, the control plane may wake at quorum but must refuse publication for this batch because reconciliation finds unresolved identities and an unknown identity.

If partial_policy is later decided as allow_incomplete, the control plane may publish an incomplete report after quorum wake that explicitly marks 2 failed expected identities, 1 timed-out expected identity, 1 missing expected identity, and 1 unknown identity.

open_points.question: Should duplicate terminal results for the same expected child identity be rejected or tolerated during reporting?

scenarios.expected: All 10 expected child identities eventually return known terminal results with no duplicates or unknown identities; control plane wakes once 6 expected terminals are observed, and final reconciliation can mark the batch complete only after all 10 expected identities are represented and no unresolved identities remain.

Using the described batch state, quorum wakes at 6 successes, but final reconciliation finds 10 expected identities versus 9 represented expected identities because the unknown identity does not match the launch snapshot; one expected identity remains unresolved, so the batch cannot receive a complete label.

If any expected child identity is reported more than once, quorum wake behavior is unchanged once 6 expected terminals exist, but final reconciliation records a duplicate and blocks the complete label unless a future duplicate_policy says otherwise.

With 2 expected child identities failed, those identities are terminal-status-accounted but remain unresolved for completeness; whether a report is publishable stays open under failure_policy, and the batch cannot be labeled complete while unresolved identities remain.

With 1 expected child identity timed_out, quorum wake behavior is unchanged after 6 expected terminals, but final reconciliation records the timed_out identity as terminal and unresolved; complete labeling is blocked.

With 1 terminal result carrying an identity outside the launch snapshot, the control plane may still wake once 6 expected identities have terminal results, but final reconciliation records an unknown identity and an expected-versus-represented mismatch; complete labeling is blocked unless the unknown is separately resolved.

For the described batch state of 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, and 1 unknown identity causing 1 missing expected identity, if partial_policy=reject or failure_policy=reject, the control plane may wake at quorum but must refuse publishing an incomplete batch report and surface unresolved reconciliation findings.

For the described batch state of 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, and 1 unknown identity causing 1 missing expected identity, if partial_policy=allow_incomplete and failure_policy permits reporting, the control plane may publish an incomplete report after wake, but it must not apply the complete label because expected_vs_represented, unknown, and unresolved checks remain unsatisfied.

scenarios.expected: All 50 expected identities are represented exactly once with terminal statuses accounted for; reconciliation can mark the batch complete and final synthesis consumes the complete summary.

One or more expected identities have no represented terminal record by the reconciliation deadline; those identities remain unresolved and the control plane records an incomplete summary pending open partial-policy handling.

One or more expected identities end in failed status; reconciliation counts the failures, lists those identities as unresolved for completeness purposes, and routes publication outcome to the open failure and partial policies.
